name: Mattermost Boards (PM fork)
description: Mattermost Boards plugin with project-management extensions: Timeline view, deadlines + DM reminders, dashboard boards, mobile login handoff.

settings.help_text: This allows board editors to share boards that can be accessed by anyone with the link.

Runs a loopback-only Model Context Protocol (MCP) server so the Mattermost Agents (AI) plugin can drive Boards on the user's behalf. Exposes seven tools: list_my_boards, get_board_info, search_cards, get_card_details, create_card, update_card, add_comment. Each call runs with the calling user's own Boards permissions — boards / cards the user can't see are filtered out. Listens on 127.0.0.1 only — never exposed off-host. To wire up: System Console → Agents → MCP → Add server, set Server URL to http://127.0.0.1:<MCPServerPort>/mcp (default http://127.0.0.1:8975/mcp). The Agents plugin auto-injects the X-Mattermost-UserID header, so each user's tool calls run as that user — you do NOT need to put a per-user token in the Headers field. Set MCP Shared Secret below if you want the endpoint gated.
TCP port for the loopback MCP listener. Only consulted when 'Enable MCP Server' is on. Pick any free port; the Server URL in the Agents plugin must match (http://127.0.0.1:<this-port>/mcp).

Optional. If set, the MCP server requires every request to carry 'Authorization: Bearer <this-value>'. Set the SAME value in the Mattermost Agents plugin under your MCP server's Headers field as a single row — Key: Authorization, Value: Bearer <paste-secret-here>. Per-user identity is handled separately via the X-Mattermost-UserID header that Agents auto-injects, so this secret is one shared value (not a Mattermost session token, not per-user). Generate any random string (e.g. `openssl rand -hex 32`). Leave empty to accept all loopback callers without auth — fine for single-user dev boxes, NOT recommended on shared servers since any local process could spoof X-Mattermost-UserID.
